source_paragraph: company goals by proposing a novel recommendation system for internal business processes, grounded in the COBIT 2019 framework. The system employs graph algorithms to map relationships, semantic similarity measure- ments to align enterprise goals with management objectives, and LSTM combined with TF-IDF methods to analyze and recommend specific activities. Experimental results show that this system optimizes IT management practices, enhances operational efficiency, and ensures regulatory compliance. Additionally, a user-friendly graphical representation of internal business pro- cesses using Neo4j enables stakeholders to visualize complex relationships within the COBIT framework, facilitating informed decision-making. This research advances IT governance practices by providing organizations with a valuable tool to automate and improve IT strategies, effectively achieving business objectives. COBIT 2019 provides a structured approach for organiza- tions to establish, implement, and maintain effective gover- nance over IT. In the context of COBIT 2019, Neo4j can be utilized to build dynamic graphical representations of various COBIT elements . By using Neo4j, organizations can create dynamic and interactive graphical models to better understand and manage the complex relationships between different COBIT 2019 elements . These results are valid for the three scenarios whether the targets are in the same beam, at the nearest neighboring beams and at the nearest co-channel beams. It shows that with the concept proposed in CC-OFDM MIMO Radar, it is possible to design a long-range radar with low power transmission by transmitting multi-orthogonal CC-OFDM symbols in series, to improve the Signal-to-Noise of the radar system.

Generally, log-based anomaly detection can be performed using either point, collective, or contextual approaches. In the log-based anomaly detection literature, most of the existing studies propose either a contextual or collective approach, where the decision is taken by examining a sequence of log events. However, detecting abnormalities in log data can be performed using a point-based approach, where one single log record is the only feature needed. Therefore, several studies explored utilising a point-based approach to detect anomalous events on logs data, such as in operating systems , , drone devices , and distribution systems , . Thus, analysing the detection result in a binary setting is impractical for the investigator. Moreover, out of all detected peculiarities, there might be several negligible ones with less severe impacts on the system or less likely to be related to an incident. source_paragraph: PREVIOUS WORKS Among the relevant published studies in the log-based anomaly detection space, sequence-based is one of the most commonly used approaches. Therefore, there are limited relevant references as this study employs a point-based approach. Below are the relevant baselines from previous research: 1) Pylogsentiment is the first study which employs sentiment analysis-based anomaly detection on operat- ing system logs using GRU and GloVe embedding. 2) SentiLog , similar to , use a two-layered BiLSTM and GloVe embedding model to perform anomaly detection on parallel file system logs. 3) TransSentLog is a further development of and uses a two-layered transformer encoder which used two attention heads and GloVe embedding along with integrated gradients to add explainability to the trained model.

CONCLUSION AND FUTURE WORK In this paper, we demonstrate how to train a log-based anomaly detection model that can prioritise the prediction of higher severity anomalies on drone flight logs while increasing the prediction confidence score at the same time. Considering the nature of the dataset, where samples that belong to different severity levels share common features, a multitask vector label representation along with severity-oriented decoding is proposed. An extensive experiment proved that the proposed approach is better than the previous work baselines while being inferior to our baseline scenario based on the accuracy and F1

source_paragraph: research introduces a novel Activity Recom- mendation System built on the COBIT 2019 framework tailored for Financial Balance Scorecard. The motivation stems from the challenges faced by auditors when identifying essential activities aligned with strategy of a company, often hindered by manual COBIT 2019 guideline navigation. To address this, a system was developed, where inputting the company strategy, triggers the display of recommended activities automatically. The methodology involved creating interconnected nodes from COBIT 2019 Element in Neo4J. This research uses a real strategy plan case study from 3 different companies as input, followed by a preprocessing step, then compares it to Enterprise Goals using TF-IDF and cosine similarity. Lastly, it facilitates compliance with data privacy regulations and industry standards, ensuring transparency and accountabil- ity. Among these frameworks, COBIT is globally recognized for overseeing Enterprise Information and Technology (I&T) comprehensively, covering all systems regardless of their lo- cation within the organization. source_paragraph: I. INTRODUCTION I. INTRODUCTION The use of drones has become increasingly common in various applications, including aerial photography, surveying, and monitoring. However, drones can also be involved in incidents such as accidents, violations, or even crimes, making it necessary to investigate drone logs as part of forensic investigations. In a forensic investigation, drone logs are essential sources of information that can help investigators reconstruct the events leading up to an incident. Drone logs typically include information such as flight paths, altitude, speed, battery life, sensor data, and other telemetry information . Important analysis of drone logs involves the identification of the most critical pieces of information in the log data and the analysis of how they relate to the incident

source_paragraph: presented a novel multi-head attention mechanism that uses various atten- tion functions to capture diverse contextual elements. The multi-head attention approach allows attention to be simul- taneously allocated to information from different subspaces and representational positions. In contrast to soft attention, which focuses attention softly on the entire input to produce a context representation with weights determined based on the relevance of each part of the input, soft attention relies on only one attention mechanism. The Python development environ- ment has been used to downsample data to 128 Hz, remove EOG artifacts, and perform bandpass frequency filtering from 4–45 Hz. The raw EEG data is transformed into a spectrogram image using the STFT method and stored as RGB images. This is done using the MATLAB (octave and oc2py) library in Python. source_paragraph: DATASET The DEAP dataset has 32 participants with a composition of 50% male and 50% female samples . The stimulus used is a video with a duration of 60 seconds. The results of the EEG recording have a duration of 63 seconds, of which 3 seconds are the baseline. One participant has 40 recordings of the EEG trials. The results of the EEG trial recordings have been mea- sured using the Self-Assessment Manikin (SAM). SAM has four emotional dimensions, namely valence, arousal, domi- nance, and liking, with ratings 0-9. For valence and arousal classes, emotion labels are divided into two categories: low and high. In a forensic setting, analysing a large number of log entries resulting from binary anomaly detection is considered inefficient and labour-intensive as the detection is performed on the acquired evidence at one time . Therefore, too many detected anomalies need to be detailed into a more fine- grained detection to help the investigator better prioritise the analysis objective. As a result, this study extends the binary anomaly detection to multi-class anomaly detection, as exemplified by another domain application such as plane flight data , image data , and internet of vehicle . For instance, if a user types the wrong password when trying to gain access to an operating system prompt, the generated log message can be considered as an anomaly , , . source_paragraph: align with the anomaly severity context, where the distance between true and predicted labels matters. If the predicted label is lower than the true label, we define it False Positive (FP). Conversely, if the true label is lower than the predicted label, we define it False Negative (FN). In this context, having less FP is more important than having less FN, as the task objective is anomaly severity detection, where we aim to detect all abnormalities along with the severity level. Therefore, undetected abnormalities, or detecting higher severity as lower ones, indicate that the model fails to meet the task’s objectives. Despite the promising results, the model’s generality remains untested as the training dataset contains only log messages acquired from 82 drone models, mostly from DJI. This is one of the main challenges in drone forensics research, where human-readable flight log datasets from other drone models are unavailable. In the future, we plan to explore the flight logs of other drone models to train more general anomaly detection models. As for the method, multi- objective learning that employs multiple loss functions to train an anomaly detection model will be explored. CONCLUSION This research demonstrates that the YOLOv8 model, specifically the YOLOv8m variant, provides excellent results in detecting Peritoneal Carcinomatosis (PC) from the used laparoscopic image dataset. By utilizing and fine-tuning the pre-trained YOLOv8 weights, this model shows superior performance with a mean Average Precision (mAP50) of 0.871, precision of 0.893, recall of 0.785, and an F1 score of 0.84. The predicted images also show that the performance of YOLOv8 with pre-trained weights is able to predict images well. All YOLOv8 scales are accurate in making predictions, with no errors in image prediction. In contrast, without using pre-trained weights, YOLOv8n and YOLOv8s still make errors in prediction, with some lesions being missed.
